chief_complaint: A 23-year-old male patient presented with acute ischemic stroke.
history: The patient had a familial history of early-onset stroke and demonstrated non-atherosclerotic vasculopathy on cerebral angiography. A comprehensive pedigree investigation uncovered a history of stroke among several family members.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Cerebral angiography showed non-atherosclerotic vasculopathy, MRI was performed, and clinical exome sequencing identified a heterozygous missense variation, c.1867 C> T (p.Thr623Met), in the KRIT1 gene.
